Which layer in a CNN is primarily responsible for reducing the spatial dimensions (width and height) of the input data?
When classifying environmental sounds, why is audio often converted into a Mel Spectrogram?

options.A: To reduce the file size of the audio
options.B: To transform the 1D audio signal into a 2D image-like representation for CNNs
options.D: To convert the audio into text

breakdown.question: In Transfer Learning, what does "freezing" the base model layers (like MobileNetV2) achieve?

options.A: It stops the model from making predictions
options.B: It prevents the pre-trained weights from being updated during training
options.C: It increases the learning rate automatically
options.D: It deletes the weights to start from scratch

breakdown.question: Which activation function is most commonly used in the final layer of a multi-class classification model to output probabilities?
What is the main advantage of MobileNetV2 over larger models like ResNet50 for a Smart Home Assistant?

options.A: It has higher accuracy on all datasets
options.B: It is lightweight and optimized for real-time performance on mobile/embedded devices
options.C: It does not require any training data
options.D: It only works with audio data

breakdown.question: Which pagination type allows you to "jump" directly to Page 10 by simply changing a number in the web address?
Why is "Infinite Scroll" more difficult to scrape than standard numbered pagination?

options.B: The URL stays the same, and content only loads when a scroll event is triggered
options.D: It requires a paid API key

breakdown.question: In "Cursor-Based Pagination," what is a "cursor"?

options.A: The mouse pointer on the screen
options.C: A unique ID or token that acts as a "bookmark" for the next set of data
options.D: A JavaScript function that clicks buttons

breakdown.question: Which tool is best suited for scraping a website that uses "Load More" buttons?
What is the main benefit of Client-Side Pagination for a user?

options.A: It keeps the server from crashing
options.B: It allows for instant page switching because all data is pre-loaded in the browser
options.C: It hides the data from web scrapers
options.D: It makes the initial page load much faster

breakdown.question: In your Flask code, what does jsonify(rates) do?

options.A: It prints the rates to the console
options.B: It converts a Python dictionary into a JSON-formatted response
options.C: It saves the rates to a database
options.D: It checks if the rates are valid numbers

breakdown.question: Which HTTP method is typically used to "retrieve" data from a REST API endpoint like /rates?
What happens if you run a Flask script and see ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'flask'?

options.A: Your code has a syntax error
options.B: Flask is not installed in your current Python environment
options.C: The server is already running on that port
options.D: Your Python version is too old

breakdown.question: In a REST API, what does the status code 404 represent?
What is the purpose of the @app.route("/convert/<from_currency>/...") decorator?

options.B: To map a specific URL pattern to a Python function
options.C: To create a new database table
